title: ping 8.8.8.8 fails when using bridge network
body:
### Issue Description

I'm using NixOS to create wireguard client. I'm using `lscr.io/linuxserver/wireguard:latest` image. I noticed that I can't connect to the wireguard hub correctly at times. Upon running `podman exec wireguard ping 8.8.8.8` it pings and getting the response one time and after that it hangs forever. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a bridge network

```
podman network create \
  --driver bridge \
  --subnet 10.89.0.0/24 \
  --gateway 10.89.0.1 \
  my-custom-net

podman run -d \
  --name wireguard2 \
  --cap-add NET_ADMIN \
  --cap-add SYS_MODULE \
  --cap-add NET_RAW \
  --network my-custom-net \
  -e PUID=1000 \
  -e PGID=1000 \
  -e TZ=Etc/UTC \
  -v ./wg0.conf:/config/wg_confs/wg0.conf:Z \
  --restart=unless-stopped \
  lscr.io/linuxserver/wireguard:latest && 

```


### Describe the results you received

```
podman run -d \
        --name wireguard2 \
        --cap-add NET_ADMIN \
        --cap-add SYS_MODULE \
        --cap-add NET_RAW \
        --network my-custom-net \
        -e PUID=1000 \
        -e PGID=1000 \
        -e TZ=Etc/UTC \
        -v ./wg0.conf:/config/wg_confs/wg0.conf:Z \
        --restart=unless-stopped \
        lscr.io/linuxserver/wireguard:latest && 
      podman exec wireguard2 ping 8.8.8.8



9909efac909be9c2084c186ffbbc4c559abae6a9fecf6d458add77d13604cbe7
PING 8.8.8.8 (8.8.8.8) 56(84) bytes of data.
64 bytes from 8.8.8.8: icmp_seq=1 ttl=254 time=59.4 ms
```

After the first response (`64 bytes from 8.8.8.8: icmp_seq=1 ttl=254 time=59.4 ms`) the command hangs forever.

However, when I switch the network to `host`, I can ping fine.

```
podman run -d \
    --name wireguard2 \
    --cap-add NET_ADMIN \
    --cap-add SYS_MODULE \
    --cap-add NET_RAW \
    --network host \
    -e PUID=1000 \
    -e PGID=1000 \
    -e TZ=Etc/UTC \
    -v ./wg0.conf:/config/wg_confs/wg0.conf:Z \
    --restart=unless-stopped \
    lscr.io/linuxserver/wireguard:latest && 
podman exec wireguard2 ping 8.8.8.8

PING 8.8.8.8 (8.8.8.8) 56(84) bytes of data.
64 bytes from 8.8.8.8: icmp_seq=1 ttl=116 time=59.6 ms
64 bytes from 8.8.8.8: icmp_seq=2 ttl=116 time=58.4 ms
64 bytes from 8.8.8.8: icmp_seq=3 ttl=116 time=54.9 ms
64 bytes from 8.8.8.8: icmp_seq=4 ttl=116 time=44.1 ms
64 bytes from 8.8.8.8: icmp_seq=5 ttl=116 time=52.8 ms
```


### Describe the results you expected

ping should work fine without any issue

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

ping does not work after restart consistently. However, I also used home-manager options to create the container as well. It uses podman quadlet in the background and when systemd service is restarted it sometimes ping works. I already have created an issue in linuxserver repository but by the look of it, this is not an issue in the image.
https://github.com/linuxserver/docker-wireguard/issues/388


comments.body:
Does it work with `--network pasta`? How does the wireguard config look like?
Can you trace the packages with something like tcpdump and see where they get dropped, something like`podman unshare --rootless-netns tcpdump -nn -i any` might be useful.

1) Adding `--network pasta` hangs similar to any other custom bridge network.

2) My wireguard config is this (I'm using home-manager to create the files with secrets safely). But for this I created a conf file by replacing all the placeholders with actual data. 

```
  sops.templates."wg0.conf" = {
    content = ''
      [Interface]
      Address = 10.0.0.2/32
      PrivateKey = ${private_key}
      ListenPort = 51820
      DNS = 10.0.0.1
  
      [Peer]
      PublicKey = hOe6V7o6CThIGDjqsAppjH54xT/koxUDPTi+5NC89E4=
      PresharedKey = ${preshared_key}
      Endpoint = ${ip}:${port}
      AllowedIPs = 0.0.0.0/0
      PersistentKeepalive = 25
    '';
  }
```

3) I don't really understand networking but I guess I'm supposed to run `podman unshare --rootless-netns tcpdump -nn -i any` and run the previous command to `ping 8.8.8.8` in the podman container?

Terminal 1: No tcp packets sent

```
podman unshare --rootless-netns tcpdump -nn -i any
tcpdump: WARNING: any: That device doesn't support promiscuous mode
(Promiscuous mode not supported on the "any" device)
tcpdump: verbose output suppressed, use -v[v]... for full protocol decode
listening on any, link-type LINUX_SLL2 (Linux cooked v2), snapshot length 262144 bytes
```

Terminal 2: As usual this hangs

```
podman run -d \
            --name wireguard2 \
            --cap-add NET_ADMIN \
            --cap-add SYS_MODULE \
            --cap-add NET_RAW \
            --network pasta \
            -e PUID=1000 \
            -e PGID=1000 \
            -e TZ=Etc/UTC \
            -v ./wg0.conf:/config/wg_confs/wg0.conf:Z \
            --restart=unless-stopped \
            lscr.io/linuxserver/wireguard:latest && 
      podman exec wireguard2 ping 8.8.8.8

6f58180d4f5704e9e133a466b21b8d4cc72c36d464c0969e8e5b45dc0b21d018
PING 8.8.8.8 (8.8.8.8) 56(84) bytes of data.
```

Even if I do `--network host` and `ping` works, `tcpdump` prints nothing. May be it's unrelated because it's running as host?

`podman unshare --rootless-netns tcpdump -nn -i any` only runs inside the rootless netns which is only used when using a custom network like in your original report. Then it should definitely show packages.

Also note SYS_MODULE cannot do anything as rootless as loading kernel modules is obviously a privileged operation, so the wireguard kernel module must already be loaded on the host.


Does it work with `--network slirp4netns`, if it does it is likely a pasta issue. For pasta you could run with `--network pasta:--pcap,/tmp/packets.pcap` to create a pcap file which show the packages going through pasta and that might be good enough for the pasta maitnainers to figure out what is going wrong.

Also what is the Endpoint ip here? An actual remote host and not the the container host? BEcause the host ip will not be reachable by default from within the container see https://blog.podman.io/2024/10/podman-5-3-changes-for-improved-networking-experience-with-pasta/

1) Noted! I loaded the wireguard kernel module in host.

```bash
❯ lsmod | rg wireguard
wireguard             122880  0
curve25519_x86_64      36864  1 wireguard
libchacha20poly1305    20480  1 wireguard
libcurve25519_generic    45056  2 curve25519_x86_64,wireguard
ip6_udp_tunnel         16384  1 wireguard
udp_tunnel             36864  1 wireguard
```

Then I installed slirp4netns and ran,

```
podman stop wireguard2 &&
podman rm wireguard2 &&
podman run -d \
  --name wireguard2 \
  --cap-add NET_ADMIN \
  --cap-add NET_RAW \
  --network slirp4netns \
  -e PUID=1000 \
  -e PGID=1000 \
  -e TZ=Etc/UTC \
  -v ./wg0.conf:/config/wg_confs/wg0.conf:Z \
  --restart=unless-stopped \
  lscr.io/linuxserver/wireguard:latest && 
podman exec wireguard2 ping 8.8.8.8

wireguard2
wireguard2
6f9adcc8e34a5f0c724af18e798669f3b46ca97ce2a3e8d18e49a8b89e892ad0
PING 8.8.8.8 (8.8.8.8) 56(84) bytes of data.
64 bytes from 8.8.8.8: icmp_seq=1 ttl=255 time=118 ms
```

This still hangs.

---

```
podman rm wireguard2 &&
podman run -d \

podman exec wireguard2 ping 8.8.8.8

wireguard2

64 bytes from 8.8.8.8: icmp_seq=1 ttl=255 time=84.5 ms
```

```
❯ tcpdump -nn -r /tmp/packets.pcap
reading from file /tmp/packets.pcap, link-type EN10MB (Ethernet), snapshot length 65535
17:04:33.082385 IP6 fe80::7440:d4ff:fe48:3900 > ff02::2: ICMP6, router solicitation, length 16
17:04:33.082406 IP6 fe80::7440:d4ff:fe48:3900 > ff02::16: HBH ICMP6, multicast listener report v2, 3 group record(s), length 68
17:04:33.291333 ARP, Request who-has 192.168.1.1 tell 192.168.1.110, length 28
17:04:33.291375 ARP, Reply 192.168.1.1 is-at 9a:55:9a:55:9a:55, length 28

17:04:33.375754 IP 8.8.8.8 > 192.168.1.110: ICMP echo reply, id 38, seq 1, length 64
17:04:33.417456 IP6 fe80::7440:d4ff:fe48:3900 > ff02::16: HBH ICMP6, multicast listener report v2, 3 group record(s), length 68
17:04:33.621830 IP 192.168.1.110.51820 > 64.225.84.64.51820: UDP, length 148
17:04:33.673453 IP6 fe80::7440:d4ff:fe48:3900 > ff02::16: HBH ICMP6, multicast listener report v2, 4 group record(s), length 88
17:04:33.673470 IP6 fe80::7440:d4ff:fe48:3900 > ff02::2: ICMP6, router solicitation, length 16
17:04:33.676503 IP6 fe80::7440:d4ff:fe48:3900 > ff02::16: HBH ICMP6, multicast listener report v2, 1 group record(s), length 28
17:04:33.960801 IP6 fe80::7440:d4ff:fe48:3900 > ff02::16: HBH ICMP6, multicast listener report v2, 4 group record(s), length 88
17:04:33.992850 IP6 fe80::7440:d4ff:fe48:3900 > ff02::16: HBH ICMP6, multicast listener report v2, 1 group record(s), length 28
17:04:37.384699 IP6 fe80::7440:d4ff:fe48:3900 > ff02::2: ICMP6, router solicitation, length 16
17:04:44.744564 IP6 fe80::7440:d4ff:fe48:3900 > ff02::2: ICMP6, router solicitation, length 16
17:04:59.592504 IP6 fe80::7440:d4ff:fe48:3900 > ff02::2: ICMP6, router solicitation, length 16
17:05:29.800666 IP6 fe80::7440:d4ff:fe48:3900 > ff02::2: ICMP6, router solicitation, length 16
```
@sbrivio-rh PTAL, are you aware of any udp/wireguard problems in pasta?

> [@sbrivio-rh](https://github.com/sbrivio-rh) PTAL, are you aware of any udp/wireguard problems in pasta?

No, not really, I'm aware of a number of users but no known issue like this one. I still have to look into it.

```
17:04:33.291445 IP 192.168.1.110 > 8.8.8.8: ICMP echo request, id 38, seq 1, length 64
17:04:33.336512 IP6 fe80::7440:d4ff:fe48:3900 > ff02::16: HBH ICMP6, multicast listener report v2, 3 group record(s), length 68
17:04:33.375754 IP 8.8.8.8 > 192.168.1.110: ICMP echo reply, id 38, seq 1, length 64
17:04:33.417456 IP6 fe80::7440:d4ff:fe48:3900 > ff02::16: HBH ICMP6, multicast listener report v2, 3 group record(s), length 68
17:04:33.621830 IP 192.168.1.110.51820 > 64.225.84.64.51820: UDP, length 148
```

AFAIK the reason the first ping appears to work is because most likely the wireguard tunnel wasn't setup and it just directly talks to 8.8.8.8 which should not be the case on a working wg0 (assuming you setup the routing right). My guess is that is because you run with `-d` and didn't wait for the container process to fully configure the interface/routing.
Afterwards it seems to send a udp package to the vpm endpoint but there is never any reply logged. So I would guess the wg handshake fails for some reason.

At this point I have no freaking clue what works and what does not. When it works every combination works when it does not nothing works and it's totally random.

This is me using the same configuration but restarting the wireguard service. I have 50 seconds before pinging the host `10.0.0.1`. One time it works, then it does not. Host is working consistently and I've been using the same host for more than a year on Ubuntu-rockchip with Docker. Never had any issues.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6a441a9d-32d3-45ea-a1f3-4a74c8194552

I feel like this occurs when I add a network to the container. Following is the generated systemd service file.

Since home-manager do not allow rootful, I installed [quadlet-nix](https://github.com/SEIAROTg/quadlet-nix) and used the same configuration in rootless mode and rootful mode (two different ports). Rootful container can hit 10.0.0.1 consistently while rootless has the same issue. 

Rootful:

```
❯ sudo podman exec -it wireguard ping -c 5 10.0.0.1
PING 10.0.0.1 (10.0.0.1) 56(84) bytes of data.
64 bytes from 10.0.0.1: icmp_seq=1 ttl=64 time=375 ms
64 bytes from 10.0.0.1: icmp_seq=2 ttl=64 time=395 ms
64 bytes from 10.0.0.1: icmp_seq=3 ttl=64 time=273 ms
64 bytes from 10.0.0.1: icmp_seq=4 ttl=64 time=147 ms

```

Rootless:

```
❯ podman exec -it wireguard ping -c 5 10.0.0.1
PING 10.0.0.1 (10.0.0.1) 56(84) bytes of data.

--- 10.0.0.1 ping statistics ---
5 packets transmitted, 0 received, 100% packet loss, time 4093ms
```

In pasta's packet capture, you have:

```
17:04:33.621830 IP 192.168.1.110.51820 > 64.225.84.64.51820: UDP, length 148
```

which is probably the first packet of the WireGuard handshake, but no reply is ever seen. I wonder if that packet gets out of the host at all. Could you take captures on the host (you can select just UDP on port 51820) at the same time, with the custom bridged network first, and with `--network=host` later, and compare them?

Is that packet going out? Does it get a reply?

I'm kinda hitting the same issue and I'm not even sure when this started.
See [Container can't connect to VPN if I don't use Network=pasta:[OPTIONS] #26499](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/26499).

I tried `podman system reset` and the container starts just fine. After a while it restarts on his own and VPN connection via WireGuard does not work anymore.

As mentioned in the thread I opened, it seems container runs fine if I pass the settings to pasta so that it emulates slirp4netns (`Network=pasta:--ipv4-only,-a,10.0.2.0,-n,24,-g,10.0.2.2,--dns-forward,10.0.2.3,-m,1500,--no-ndp,--no-dhcpv6,--no-dhcp`). However, the VPN connection does not longer work if I join a custom podman network.

I've been using podman with pasta for quite some time now and has been working fine. This started recently.

@sbrivio-rh have you found anything?
As a workaround, I've been using rootful wireguard and used `host.containers.internal` to reverse proxy to rootless containers. It's been working good so far.
